hypothesis: Fine-tuning the transformer model on a small set of experimentally measured ATR-IR mixture spectra with known compositions—where non-linear spectral interactions between components are present—will yield greater performance improvements for chemically reactive or hydrogen-bonding compound pairs (such as acid-amine mixtures) than for non-interacting pairs, because the linear superposition approximation used in synthetic training data systematically fails for such systems, and a domain-adaptive fine-tuning strategy that explicitly encodes pairwise molecular interaction descriptors as additional conditioning inputs will recover this performance gap more effectively than spectral fine-tuning alone.
